dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.text: Good morning! You're up early today.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.text: Yes, I'm cutting the hedge. It's too high now. The work needs to be done before summer.

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.text: It looks good. Can I help you?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.text: That's very nice of you, but I'm nearly finished. Come inside. Do you want some tea or coffee?

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.text: Tea, please. Actually, I came here because I need to tell you something.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.text: That sounds serious. Let me put these down. Come in, sit at the table.

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.text: Thanks. You have a cup on the chair here.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.text: Oh, from my breakfast. I'll take it to the kitchen. So, what do you need to tell me?

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.text: I received a letter yesterday. It's from David.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.text: David? I haven't heard that name in years. How is he?

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.text: He's coming back to town next week. He wants to see you.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.text: I don't know, Sam. That was a previous relationship. It ended badly.

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.text: I know. But maybe he wants to say sorry? People can change.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.text: He left me alone when I needed him most. I was in the hospital, and he never came.

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.text: That's true. I remember. But will you tell him you don't want to meet? Or will you just not respond?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.text: I'm not sure. This is a difficult problem. What do you think I should do?

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.text: Well, maybe you can meet him once. Just for coffee. You don't need to be close friends.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.text: You're probably right. I can't deal with the past if I never face it.

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.text: Exactly. And if it doesn't go well, you can leave early. I can come with you if you want.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.text: No, it's okay. I should do this myself. But thank you for your help and for telling me.

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.text: That's what friends are for. Will you tell him yes today?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.text: Yes, I'll send him an email this afternoon. Now, let me make that tea. Do you want some cake too?

dialogue.speaker: Sam
dialogue.text: That sounds perfect. And Alex? I'm proud of you. This takes courage.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.text: Thanks, Sam. I'm glad you came here today.
